description_long: This house features a two-story structure with a combination of stone and brick exterior walls, topped by a reddish-brown hip roof. The front facade is distinguished by two prominent balconies supported by thick columns, each with wooden railings and large wooden doors beneath. The upper story displays a symmetrical arrangement of small, square windows, while the lower level has larger doors and fewer windows. The roof is multi-tiered, with the main section covering the central block and lower sections over the side wings. The overall design suggests a Mediterranean or Romanesque influence, with rustic textures and earthy tones. The house appears robust and well-suited for an urban neighborhood setting.
description: A two-story stone and brick house with a reddish hip roof, prominent balconies, and symmetrical window placement. The design features rustic textures and Mediterranean architectural elements.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the plain stone and brick walls, small square windows, and the hip roof without visible entryways or balconies.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting two large balconies with wooden railings, thick supporting columns, three main entry doors, and symmetrical window placement.
description_view_2: Opposite rear view, similar to view 0, with plain walls, small windows, and no visible entry features.
description_view_3: Alternate front view, nearly identical to view 1, showing the main entry, balconies, and architectural details from a slightly different angle.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry features three wooden doors beneath two balconies, supported by thick columns and accented with wooden railings.
